intent: Register a new app_icons entry for habit_stack (a new Flutter app) so it gets an on-style launcher icon from the shared family, and whitelist its package id in phone_focus_mode so the app isn't killed on-device.
scope: No changes to any other package's behavior
changes: Added a new chain-link glyph (_CHAIN_LINK) to glyphs.py: two interlocking stadium-ring outlines, sized so the 72px stroke leaves a genuinely hollow interior at launcher size
Registered habit_stack in apps.py's APPS dict (accent #66BB6A matching the app's own ThemeData seedColor, glyph chain-link, icon_name habit-stack, linux=False)

Added com.kuhy.habit_stack to phone_focus_mode/config.sh in both places a companion-app package id needs to appear (the always-on productive-apps whitelist and the night/curfew health-tool whitelist), then redeployed to the phone

verification.evidence: 877 passed in 1.91s; TOTAL coverage 100.00% (2816 stmts, 758 branches, 0 missed). Note: an earlier run against the pyenv global Python (not this repo's .venv) spuriously failed on a missing markdownify import in the unrelated archwiki_rag package -- confirmed pre-existing and environment-only, not a real gap; re-running via .venv resolved it.

Visual review of the generated contact sheet (full size, circle-masked, 48dp) confirmed the chain-link glyph reads clearly as two interlocking rings at all three sizes, no safe-box warnings from the CLI
Deploy completed successfully; config.sh (including the new com.kuhy.habit_stack entries) pushed to device and daemon restarted

risks: New glyph geometry was hand-derived (stroke width vs. ring interior hollow space); mitigated by visual review of the actual rendered preview sheet before committing to the design

phone_focus_mode/config.sh is a live-deployed config; a bad edit could affect other whitelisted apps -- mitigated by adding only new lines, never touching existing entries, and confirming via deploy.sh's own status output that other apps' whitelist state was unaffected
rollback: Re-run PYTHONPATH=~/testsAndMisc python3 -m python_pkg.app_icons generate --app habit_stack --android is then a no-op since the app entry no longer exists; the habit_stack repo's already-generated assets/icon/ files are unaffected (that's a separate repo)
Redeploy phone_focus_mode (ADB_SERIAL=BL9000EEA0000102 bash phone_focus_mode/deploy.sh) to push the reverted config.sh back to the phone
